AcquisitionSourceEnum.description: How a :class:`~app.models.grade.GradedCard` entered a user's collection.

AnnouncementRead.description: Global banner shown to every user when enabled.

Pipeline stage of a single job application.

Each transition is recorded as an ``ApplicationEvent`` so the
applicant can be kept informed and the portal shows a full history.

AuditFacets.title: AuditFacets

BanRequest.description: Ban a user with an optional reason shown in the audit trail.

BlogPostCreate.description: Admin: create a post. `slug` derives from the title when omitted.
BlogPostCreate.title: BlogPostCreate

BlogPostUpdate.description: Admin: partial update. Send only the fields that changed.
BlogPostUpdate.title: BlogPostUpdate

BlogStatusEnum.description: Publication lifecycle of a blog post.
BlogStatusEnum.title: BlogStatusEnum

image.description: JPEG/PNG photo of a single card.
image.title: Image

CardAnalytics.description: Derived market metrics for one card (public, read-only).
CardAnalytics.title: CardAnalytics

CardHolding.description: One owned copy of a card (a `GradedCard`), with derived figures.
CardHolding.title: CardHolding

CardOwnership.description: Rolled-up ownership of one card for the signed-in user.
CardOwnership.title: CardOwnership

CheckoutRequest.description: Start a Pro checkout for the given billing interval.
CheckoutRequest.title: CheckoutRequest

EmploymentTypeEnum.description: Employment basis for a job posting.
EmploymentTypeEnum.title: EmploymentTypeEnum

EntitlementsRead.description: Everything the client needs to render gates, badges, and the paywall.
EntitlementsRead.title: EntitlementsRead

FeatureFlagUpsert.description:
Set a flag's enabled state by key, creating it if missing.

Backs the in-app inspect overlay, which toggles a component's flag
without knowing its id. `label` is used only when the flag is created.
FeatureFlagUpsert.title: FeatureFlagUpsert

GradeHouseEnum.description: Recognised grading houses (Loupe is our first-party grade).
GradeHouseEnum.title: GradeHouseEnum

GradedCardCreate.description:
Body for manually creating a graded card (admin / import flows).

Either ``card_id`` (an existing local UUID) or ``upstream_id`` (a
composite ``<source>:<external_id>`` from a catalog hit / scan /
deep link) must be supplied. When ``upstream_id`` is used, the
server materializes the local :class:`Card` + :class:`CardExternalRef`
via :func:`card_resolver_service.ensure_local_card` before inserting
the grade — so the user never has to think about ids.

condition.description: PSA-style raw condition (nm/lp/mp/hp/dmg). Only meaningful for ungraded (`house=loupe`) cards; ignored for slabbed grades.

GradedCardCreate.title: GradedCardCreate

GradedCardRead.description: Public representation of a graded card.
GradedCardRead.title: GradedCardRead

GradedCardUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /v1/grades/{id}``.

Every field is optional — send only the ones you want to change. We
accept ``grade`` / ``house`` / ``subgrades`` here (and not just the
cost-basis trio) so the same form can be used to correct manually
entered holdings, e.g. after the user gets a slab back from PSA.
GradedCardUpdate.title: GradedCardUpdate

HealthCheck.description: One probe in the health report (DB, migrations, Redis, a provider, …).
HealthCheck.title: HealthCheck

HealthReport.description: Aggregate health — the worst check sets the headline status.
HealthReport.title: HealthReport

client_provider.description: Identifier for the on-device engine, e.g. "apple_vision" / "mlkit".
client_provider.title: Client Provider

ocr_confidence.description: Mean confidence reported by the on-device OCR.
ocr_confidence.title: Ocr Confidence

text.description: Raw OCR text the client extracted on-device.
text.title: Text

card_id.description: Local catalog UUID (string) when the card has been materialized.
card_id.title: Card Id

chosen_card_id.description: Optional correction. May be a local UUID or composite upstream_id string. Required when ``correct`` is False to drive future re-ranks.
chosen_card_id.title: Chosen Card Id

correct.description: True = the top candidate matched the real card.
correct.title: Correct

IdentifyParsed.description: Structured fields extracted from OCR text. All optional.
IdentifyParsed.title: IdentifyParsed

accuracy_score.description: Confidence of the top candidate, 0 if none were found.
accuracy_score.title: Accuracy Score

fallback_reason.description: Human-readable reason when fallback_required is true.
fallback_reason.title: Fallback Reason

fallback_required.description: When true, the server refused to call the paid OCR provider (typically because OCR_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD is exhausted). The client should run on-device OCR (Apple Vision / ML Kit) and resubmit via POST /v1/cards/identify/text.
fallback_required.title: Fallback Required

ImpersonateResult.description: A short-lived access token for viewing the app as another user.

Publication lifecycle of a job posting.

``open`` postings are visible on the public careers page and accept
applications; ``draft`` is admin-only; ``closed`` stays in the portal
for record-keeping but no longer accepts applications.
JobStatusEnum.title: JobStatusEnum

LoginResult.description:
Response for ``POST /v1/auth/login``.

Backward-compatible superset of :class:`TokenPair`: on a normal sign-in the
token fields are populated exactly as before (``mfa_required`` is ``False``).
When the account has two-factor enabled, the tokens are withheld and
``mfa_required``/``mfa_token`` drive a second step at ``/auth/mfa/verify``.

mfa_required.description: True when a second factor is needed.
mfa_required.title: Mfa Required

mfa_token.description: Short-lived token to POST to /auth/mfa/verify with a code.
mfa_token.title: Mfa Token

LoginResult.title: LoginResult

MfaCodeRequest.description: Body for enable/disable — a single TOTP or backup code.
MfaCodeRequest.title: MfaCodeRequest

MfaEnableResponse.description: One-time backup codes, shown once after enabling MFA.
MfaEnableResponse.title: MfaEnableResponse

qr_svg.description: Inline data:image/svg+xml QR of the URI.
qr_svg.title: Qr Svg

MfaStatusResponse.description: Whether the signed-in user has two-factor enabled.
MfaStatusResponse.title: MfaStatusResponse

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.
total.title: Total

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.
total.title: Total

PlanConfigRead.description: The Loupe Pro plan shape the admin controls live.
PlanConfigRead.title: PlanConfigRead

PlanFeatures.description: Boolean capability gates the UI reads to lock/unlock surfaces.
PlanFeatures.title: PlanFeatures

PresignedUpload.description: A single presigned PUT URL the client uses to upload one angle.
PresignedUpload.title: PresignedUpload

PriceOverrideRequest.description: Manually record a price point (stored as a `manual`-source snapshot).
PriceOverrideRequest.title: PriceOverrideRequest

RawConditionEnum.description:
PSA-style condition grades for RAW (ungraded) cards.

Optional on every :class:`~app.models.grade.GradedCard` row — only
meaningful when the card has not been slabbed by a third-party grading
house. Mirrors the standard TCG/sports vocabulary collectors and
eBay use so per-condition pricing maps directly.
RawConditionEnum.title: RawConditionEnum

RecentsPayload.description:
A user's recents. ``viewed`` items are stored verbatim (the client's
camelCase ``{id, name, imageUrl, setName, kind}``), so no field mapping
is needed in either direction.
RecentsPayload.title: RecentsPayload

RefundResult.description: The outcome of refunding a user's latest charge.
RefundResult.title: RefundResult

ReportDownloadResponse.description:
Response for ``GET /v1/reports/{id}/download``.

`download_url` is a short-lived presigned URL when the storage
backend supports it. When it's ``None``, the client must fall back
to the streaming endpoint (`GET /v1/reports/{id}/file`).
ReportDownloadResponse.title: ReportDownloadResponse

ReportGenerateRequest.description: Body for ``POST /v1/reports`` — generate a new statement.
ReportGenerateRequest.title: ReportGenerateRequest

ReportRead.description: Public representation of a generated portfolio statement.
ReportRead.title: ReportRead

ReportStatusEnum.description: Lifecycle of a generated portfolio statement PDF.
ReportStatusEnum.title: ReportStatusEnum

ResolveCardRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/cards/resolve``.

Provide any combination — first non-empty resolves wins (order:
``uuid`` → ``upstream_id`` → ``phash`` → ``query``).

materialize.description: When true (default), upstream-only hits are persisted as local Card + CardExternalRef rows so the user can attach grades / collection items / alerts to a stable UUID.
materialize.title: Materialize

phash.description: Perceptual image hash from a scan.
phash.title: Phash

tcg.description: Optional tcg hint to narrow free-text matches.
tcg.title: Tcg

ResolveCardRequest.title: ResolveCardRequest

RevenueMonthPoint.description: New Pro members that started in a given month (YYYY-MM).
RevenueMonthPoint.title: RevenueMonthPoint

ScanJobCreate.description: Body for ``POST /v1/scans`` — request a new scan + presigned uploads.

angles.description: Subset of card angles to upload.
angles.title: Angles

scanner_id.description: Originating scanner; null for phone scans.
scanner_id.title: Scanner Id

ScanJobCreate.title: ScanJobCreate

ScanJobRead.description: API representation of a scan job.
ScanJobRead.title: ScanJobRead

ScannerRead.description: A registered scanner as returned by the API.
ScannerRead.title: ScannerRead

ScannerTransportEnum.description: Transport protocol used by a scanner device.
ScannerTransportEnum.title: ScannerTransportEnum

SealedHoldingRead.description: A user's sealed holding plus joined product metadata.
SealedHoldingRead.title: SealedHoldingRead

SealedHoldingUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /v1/sealed-holdings/{id}``.

All fields optional. ``opened_at`` is a one-way toggle on the
client (set it to the current time when the user "rips" the box),
but we accept any timestamp here so import flows can backfill.
SealedHoldingUpdate.title: SealedHoldingUpdate

SealedMarketRead.description:
Live market snapshot for one sealed product (``GET /v1/sealed/{id}/market``).

Sealed SKUs have no stored daily history, so the snapshot is a current
TCGplayer quote (low/mid/high/market) + MSRP. ``points`` is a real value
line anchored at MSRP-on-release → current market, so the UI can chart how
the product appreciated since launch (no fabricated points).
SealedMarketRead.title: SealedMarketRead

SealedPricePoint.description: One point on a sealed product's value line.
SealedPricePoint.title: SealedPricePoint

SealedProductTypeEnum.description:
Categories of sealed TCG product collectors track as investments.

Mirrors the vocabulary the major retailers (TCGplayer, Troll & Toad)
use for filtering so external catalog imports map 1:1. Keep this
list closed — anything truly bespoke goes in ``other`` and gets
promoted in a later migration once we see real volume.
SealedProductTypeEnum.title: SealedProductTypeEnum

SiteConfigRead.description: Everything the developer-portal config page reads in one shot.
SiteConfigRead.title: SiteConfigRead

SubscriptionCancelRequest.description: Cancel a user's subscription; defaults to end-of-period (keeps Pro).
SubscriptionCancelRequest.title: SubscriptionCancelRequest

TcgEnum.description: Trading-card games supported by the catalog.
TcgEnum.title: TcgEnum

TestAccountCreated.description: A freshly-minted sandbox account — the password is shown only once.
TestAccountCreated.title: TestAccountCreated

TokenPair.description: Returned by every successful sign-in / refresh.
TokenPair.title: TokenPair

UpcomingReportRead.description:
When the next monthly / yearly statement will auto-materialise.

Returned by ``GET /v1/reports/upcoming`` so the UI can render an
Amex-style "Your next statement closes on Jun 1" line without the
client doing date math against the user's timezone.
UpcomingReportRead.title: UpcomingReportRead

UserSettingsRead.description: Per-user settings as returned by the API.
UserSettingsRead.title: UserSettingsRead

UserUpdate.description: Allowed mutations on the caller's own user record.
UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate

WaitlistJoin.description: Public: join the scanner waitlist (the checkout CTA payload).
WaitlistJoin.title: WaitlistJoin

WaitlistJoined.description: Public response after joining — the reference + the line position.
WaitlistJoined.title: WaitlistJoined

WaitlistStats.description: Public aggregate — drives the "Join N collectors" social proof.
WaitlistStats.title: WaitlistStats

WaitlistStatusEnum.description:
Pipeline stage of a Loupe Scanner waitlist signup.

``waiting`` is a fresh signup; ``invited`` means we've reached out with a
purchase link; ``purchased`` is a converted buyer (set once Stripe / the
fulfilment provider confirms an order); ``removed`` is an opt-out.
WaitlistStatusEnum.title: WaitlistStatusEnum

WaitlistStatusUpdate.description: Admin: advance a signup's pipeline stage.
WaitlistStatusUpdate.title: WaitlistStatusUpdate

WatchlistItemRead.description: One pinned card. `card_name`/`card_image_url` joined for the UI.
WatchlistItemRead.title: WatchlistItemRead

app__schemas__price__PriceSnapshotRead.title: PriceSnapshotRead

info.description:
# Loupe Backend Overview

Loupe is a card-grading platform powered by a Raspberry Pi-based desktop
scanner.  The scanner captures four angles of a trading card (front, back,
top edge, bottom edge), uploads them to this backend, and receives a
forensic grade report covering:

- **Centering** — how well the print is aligned within the card border.
- **Corners** — wear, whitening, and rounding.
- **Edges** — chipping, dings, and edge whitening.
- **Surface** — scratches, print lines, and texture defects.

The backend persists every scan, computes a perceptual fingerprint for
duplicate detection, surfaces upstream catalog data (Pokémon TCG IO,
Scryfall, YGOPRODeck), and tracks user collections.

## Architecture

```
+--------------+        +-----------------+        +----------------+
|  Loupe iOS   |  HTTPS |  FastAPI (this) |  arq   |  scan worker   |
|  + Scanner   +------->+  /v1/* + /ws    +------->+  grading + FP  |
+--------------+        +--------+--------+        +----------------+
                                  |
                  +---------------+---------------+
                  |               |               |
              Postgres        Redis (cache/      S3 / MinIO
              (async)         pub-sub/arq)       (scan images)
```

All I/O is async; the scan worker is the only place where the slow
forensic pipeline runs.  WebSocket clients receive progress events on
`/ws/scans` as the worker advances.

# API Principles

## Versioning
- All resource endpoints live under `/v1/...`.
- System endpoints (`/health`, `/version`, `/metrics`) are unversioned.

## Authentication
- Mobile clients authenticate with Apple or Google Sign-In, exchanging an
  identity token at `/v1/auth/apple` or `/v1/auth/google` for a backend
  JWT pair (`access_token`, `refresh_token`).
- All `/v1/*` endpoints (except `auth.*`) require
  `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` headers.
- Tokens are RS256-signed; ephemeral keys are generated at boot if no
  PEM is configured.  Production deployments should set
  `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM` + `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`.

## Pagination
List endpoints return `{ items, total, page, page_size }` envelopes.
Default `page_size` is 25; the maximum varies per endpoint.

## Errors
- `400` — Pydantic validation failure (body returned verbatim).
- `401` — missing/invalid bearer token.
- `403` — token valid but user lacks access to the resource.
- `404` — resource missing or not owned by the caller.
- `409` — conflict on idempotent operations.
- `5xx` — unhandled server errors; correlated with `X-Request-Id`.

## Caching
The middleware applies `Cache-Control` per path prefix.  Catalog data
(`/v1/sets`, `/v1/cards`) is cacheable for an hour; everything user-
scoped is `private, no-store`.

## Rate limiting
Enforced upstream of FastAPI (Cloudflare/NGINX).  The backend trusts
`X-Request-Id` if supplied so logs and traces share a correlation key.

# Scan → Graded Card Lifecycle

```
[POST /v1/scans]
  --> ScanJob.status = queued
  --> response: 4 presigned PUT urls

[client uploads images directly to S3]
  --> status remains queued / transitions to uploading

[POST /v1/scans/{id}/complete]
  --> status = processing
  --> arq enqueue: process_scan({job_id, user_id})

[worker]
  --> grading_service.grade_from_images()
  --> fingerprint_service.fingerprint_from_images()
  --> insert GradedCard + Fingerprint
  --> status = complete
  --> publish ScanProgressEvent on loupe:scans:user:{user_id}

[WS /ws/scans?token=...]
  --> client receives: hello, scan_progress{status=processing, progress=0.1}
                       scan_progress{status=processing, progress=0.6}
                       scan_progress{status=complete,   progress=1.0, result=...}
```

Failures move the job to `failed` and publish a terminal event with
`message` populated.  Soft-deleted graded cards keep their fingerprint
row so the duplicate detector still sees them.

# Endpoint Playbook

## First-launch sign-in
1. `POST /v1/auth/apple` with `id_token` from Sign-In with Apple.
2. Persist `access_token` (15m TTL) and `refresh_token` (30d TTL).
3. `GET /v1/me` to hydrate the profile screen.
4. `GET /v1/me/settings` to apply user preferences.

## Pair a hardware scanner
1. `POST /v1/scanners` with the device serial + nickname.
2. Scanner posts heartbeats to `POST /v1/scanners/{id}/heartbeat`
   every 30s while idle.

## Grade a card
1. `POST /v1/scans` (optionally pass `scanner_id`, `angles`).
2. Upload each angle to the returned `upload_url` via HTTP `PUT`.
3. `POST /v1/scans/{id}/complete` with the list of uploaded angles.
4. Subscribe to `wss://.../ws/scans?token=<access_token>` to receive
   live progress events; the final event contains `graded_card_id`.
5. `GET /v1/grades/{graded_card_id}` to render the grade report.

## Browse the catalog
- `GET /v1/sets?tcg=pokemon&page=1` — paginated set list.
- `GET /v1/cards?q=charizard&tcg=pokemon&page=1` — text search.
- `GET /v1/cards/{id}` — full card detail with metadata blob.

## Track value over time
- `GET /v1/prices?card_id=<id>&house=psa&grade=10` returns snapshots
  collected from supported pricing sources.

## Tag Reference

| Tag | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `auth` | Sign-In with Apple/Google exchange and refresh-token rotation. |
| `cards` | Read-only card catalog search & lookup. |
| `collections` | User-defined binders/decks with graded-card membership. |
| `grades` | User's graded-card history (soft-deletable). |
| `prices` | Historical price snapshots per card/grade/house. |
| `scanners` | Hardware scanner pairing, heartbeat, and CRUD. |
| `scans` | Scan-job ingestion (presigned uploads) + grading triggers. |
| `sets` | Read-only card-set browsing (paginated). |
| `system` | Health, version, and metrics endpoints. Unauthenticated. |
| `users` | Authenticated profile + per-user settings. |
| `ws` | WebSocket channels (live scan progress, future notifications). |

## Upstream Services

| Service | URL |
| --- | --- |
| Pokémon TCG IO | `https://api.pokemontcg.io/v2` |
| Scryfall (Magic) | `https://api.scryfall.com` |
| YGOPRODeck (Yu-Gi-Oh) | `https://db.ygoprodeck.com/api/v7` |
| Apple JWKS | `https://appleid.apple.com/auth/keys` |
| Google JWKS | `https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/certs` |
info.title: loupe-backend

get.description: Return basic process + dependency health information.

schema.title: Response List Applications V1 Admin Applications Get

get.summary: Get an application with its full event trail

patch.summary: Advance an application and notify the applicant

schema.title: Response List Posts V1 Admin Blog Get
Response Get Logs V1 Admin Cloud Logs Get

get.summary: Recent Cloud Run log entries (newest first)

patch.summary: Update the Pro plan shape (limits + per-feature gating)

get.summary: All tables with column/FK counts and row estimates
Columns, indexes, and relationships for one table

schema.title: Response List Flags V1 Admin Flags Get

put.summary: Set a flag's enabled state by key (create if missing)

post.summary: Ask a question of the database (read-only, super-admin)

schema.title: Response Insights Status V1 Admin Insights Status Get
Response List Jobs V1 Admin Jobs Get

post.summary: Get a short-lived token to view the app as a user (super-admin)

patch.summary: Comp a user to Loupe Pro (or back to free)

post.summary: Sign a user out of every device (revoke all tokens)

schema.title: Response List Waitlist V1 Admin Waitlist Get

get.description: Returns every price alert the signed-in user has created, most-recent first. Pass `pending=true` to filter out alerts that have already fired.

schema.title: Response List Mine V1 Alerts Get

get.description: Returns the data the mobile Analytics tab renders, all at once. Stats, set indexes, top movers, concentration, year and grade distributions, and grader split — computed pure-DB from the authenticated user's graded cards. No N+1, no per-card fan-out.

schema.title: Response Get Overview V1 Analytics Overview Get

get.description: Returns `{ minSupportedVersion, forceUpdate, flags, homeRails }`. `forceUpdate` is server-computed from the optional `clientVersion` query param. Clients should call this on cold start and again on resume after >1h, and persist the last response so launch is offline-tolerant.

schema.title: Response Get App Config V1 App Config Get

post.description:
Set a new password after verifying the current one. Bumps the token epoch
so every *other* session is revoked, then re-issues a fresh pair for this
device — so the user stays signed in here but is signed out everywhere else.

Find-or-create a user by email, no password required.

Gated by ``APP_ENV``: only available outside of ``production``.
post.summary: Dev-only: sign in by email without a password

post.description:
Expire the HttpOnly auth cookie. JS can't clear it, so the web calls this
on sign-out. Bearer clients (mobile) simply drop their stored token.

Bump the user's token epoch, instantly invalidating *all* of their
outstanding access + refresh tokens (every device). The kill switch for a
lost device or a stolen token. Also clears this browser's cookie.
post.summary: Revoke every session for the signed-in user
Confirm enrollment with a code; returns one-time backup codes
Begin 2FA enrollment — returns a secret + QR

get.summary: Whether two-factor is enabled for the signed-in user

post.description: Exchange the login ``mfa_token`` + a TOTP/backup code for a token pair.
post.summary: Complete sign-in with a 2FA code (login second step)

schema.title: Response Stripe Webhook V1 Billing Webhook Post
Response List Posts V1 Blog Posts Get

get.summary: Get a published post by slug

get.description: Legacy DB-backed paginated catalog search.

Rolling accuracy/cost metrics over the last ``days`` days.

Gated by :func:`require_admin` — caller's email must be in the
``ADMIN_EMAILS`` allowlist. Returns 403 otherwise.

post.description:
Run OCR + catalog ranking against the uploaded photo.

Always 200 unless the image is missing / unreadable / oversize.
Upstream OCR or catalog errors are absorbed by the pipeline and
surfaced as an empty / low-confidence candidate list.
post.summary: Identify a card from a photo

post.description:
Run the catalog / scoring pipeline against text the client OCR'd.

Used when ``POST /v1/cards/identify`` returned ``fallback_required=true``
(typically because the monthly Vision budget is exhausted). The
client extracts text on-device with Apple Vision or ML Kit and
POSTs it here. No paid OCR call is made; ``cost_usd`` is always 0.
post.summary: Identify a card from client-side OCR text (budget fallback)

post.description: Persist the user's correctness signal for future re-ranking.
post.summary: Submit thumbs-up / thumbs-down on a prior identification

post.description:
Single funnel: scan / search / deep-link / manual entry all land here.

Returns ``{card_id, upstream_id, source, confidence, canonical}``
where ``canonical`` is the same shape as
``GET /v1/cards/{id}/canonical`` — i.e. the user can render the
full card from this one response. Returns 404 when no path resolves.

schema.title: Response Resolve Card V1 Cards Resolve Post

post.summary: Resolve any card hint to a canonical Loupe id (public)

get.description:
Live search against Scryfall / Pokémon TCG / YGOPRODeck.

Always returns 200; upstream errors are surfaced via an ``error`` field
on an otherwise-empty envelope so the mobile client can degrade
gracefully.

schema.title: Response Search Live V1 Cards Search Get

get.description:
Mixed trending feed across the three live catalogs.

Cached for 15 minutes. Falls back to a small hardcoded set of
well-known cards if every upstream is unreachable, so the endpoint
never returns a 5xx.

schema.title: Response Get Trending V1 Cards Trending Get

get.description: Lookup by composite ``<source>:<upstream_id>`` or local UUID.

schema.title: Response Get One V1 Cards  Card Id  Get

get.description:
Derived market metrics for a card — market cap, momentum, volatility,
grade premium, all-time high/low, and liquidity — composed from the market
snapshot + recent comps so every client shows the same numbers.

Return the single "holy grail" :class:`CanonicalCard` document.

Composes catalog identity, set, images, attributes, multi-source
pricing, population, listings, comps, and (for ``psa:<cert>`` ids)
a verified cert into one object the frontend can render directly.
Section provenance is included so the UI can flag real vs
synthesized data.

schema.title: Response Get Canonical V1 Cards  Card Id  Canonical Get

get.description: Recent sold comps fanned out across configured providers.

schema.title: Response Get Comps V1 Cards  Card Id  Comps Get

get.description:
Pivot recent sold comps into a per-grade summary for the card.

Powers the price-by-grade pill row on the scan/detail sheet:
UNGRADED $X · PSA 10 $Y · BGS 10 $Z. Returns ``grades: []`` when
no comps are available so the client always renders.

schema.title: Response Get Grade Summary V1 Cards  Card Id  Grade Summary Get

get.description:
Live listings fanned out across configured providers (eBay, ...).

Returns ``listings: []`` when no provider is configured, so the
client always renders successfully.

schema.title: Response Get Listings V1 Cards  Card Id  Listings Get

get.description:
Return the full per-house × per-grade market view for a card.

Synthesizes the graded table deterministically from the live raw
market price (seeded by card id) so the page is stable across
refreshes — see :func:`app.services.market.market_service.build_market_for_card`.

schema.title: Response Get Market V1 Cards  Card Id  Market Get

get.description:
Lowest live listing per provider + deep-link search URL.

Powers the marketplace chip row on the card-detail sheet
(eBay $X · TCGplayer $Y · ...). Empty ``providers`` is rendered as
"no live listings yet" by the client.

schema.title: Response Get Marketplace Prices V1 Cards  Card Id  Marketplace Prices Get

get.description:
Facebook Marketplace listings near the user for the viewed card.

Powers the "Near You" carousel on the card-detail sheet. ``lat``/``lng``
come from the device's location (foreground permission). Returns
``listings: []`` when Apify is unconfigured or has no matches, so the
client always renders a clean empty state.

schema.title: Response Get Nearby Listings V1 Cards  Card Id  Nearby Listings Get

get.description:
Per-user ownership context: every copy the signed-in user owns of this
card, with grade/scan data and rolled-up cost basis, holding value, and
unrealized P/L. Returns ``owned=false`` when they own none.

Return a price history series for the given composite card id.

Currently synthesizes a deterministic walk around the live market
price (seeded by card id) so the chart is stable across refreshes.
When ``house``/``grade`` are supplied the walk is scaled by the same
drift × multiplier math that drives the per-house market table, so
tap-a-grade-row → chart-filters-by-tier works without a second API.

schema.title: Response Get Prices V1 Cards  Card Id  Prices Get

get.description:
One equilibrium "fair value" for the raw card (blended from sold comps,
live listings, and catalog market), the transparent input signals, and the
per-grade price ladder (PSA 10 / BGS 9.5 / …) — in a single round-trip.

schema.title: Response Get Valuation V1 Cards  Card Id  Valuation Get

get.summary: Loupe Value — equilibrium fair value + per-grade ladder (public)
Track an application (gated on the applicant's email)

schema.title: Response List Jobs V1 Careers Jobs Get

get.summary: Get an open role by slug

schema.title: Response List Mine V1 Collections Get
Response List Items V1 Collections  Collection Id  Items Get
Response Add Item V1 Collections  Collection Id  Items Post

post.summary: Add a graded card to a collection

schema.title: Response Get Flags V1 Flags Get

parameters.description: Hard cap on rows returned in a single response so large vaults don't OOM the mobile client. Defaults to 500 — more than any real collector currently owns. Combine with `cursor` to page.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Hard cap on rows returned in a single response so large vaults don't OOM the mobile client. Defaults to 500 — more than any real collector currently owns. Combine with `cursor` to page.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Zero-based offset into the sorted result set. Used together with `limit` for infinite-scroll pagination. The client should increment by `limit` between requests.
parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Zero-based offset into the sorted result set. Used together with `limit` for infinite-scroll pagination. The client should increment by `limit` between requests.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Free-text search. Case-insensitive substring match across the card name and set name. Backend search keeps mobile responsive even on 5k-card vaults where client-side filtering would stall.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Free-text search. Case-insensitive substring match across the card name and set name. Backend search keeps mobile responsive even on 5k-card vaults where client-side filtering would stall.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Filter to a single set by exact name (case-sensitive).
parameters.name: set

schema.description: Filter to a single set by exact name (case-sensitive).
schema.title: Set

parameters.description: Filter by grading house slug (e.g. `loupe`, `psa`, `bgs`). Case-insensitive — normalised to lower.
parameters.name: house

schema.description: Filter by grading house slug (e.g. `loupe`, `psa`, `bgs`). Case-insensitive — normalised to lower.
schema.title: House

parameters.description: Minimum grade (inclusive). Rows below this are dropped.
parameters.name: min_grade

schema.description: Minimum grade (inclusive). Rows below this are dropped.
schema.title: Min Grade
Response List Mine V1 Grades Get

get.description: Returns `{ range, points: [{date, priceUsd}], deltaUsd, deltaPct }`. Computed from the per-card `price_history` populated by the daily `price_backfill` worker. Empty array when the user has no graded cards or no upstream price data has been backfilled yet.

schema.title: Response Get History V1 Grades History Get

get.description: Returns `[{cardId, points: number[14], deltaPct}, ...]`. Each entry is the graded-card id (not the catalog card id) so the client can map directly to vault rows. Cards with no upstream price history yield a flat line at their current estimate.

schema.title: Response Get Sparklines V1 Grades Sparklines Get

get.description: Returns `{ totalValueUsd, cardCount, avgGrade, avgAccuracy, totalCostUsd, costBasisCardCount, unrealizedPnlUsd, unrealizedPnlPct }`. All values are computed from the user's real graded cards; `avgAccuracy` is null until the scan pipeline reports per-job accuracy. The cost-basis fields are null when no card has a recorded purchase price (so the UI can hide P/L rather than display a misleading `$0`).

schema.title: Response Get Summary V1 Grades Summary Get

get.summary: Portfolio aggregates for the signed-in user

get.description: Returns ``{ topMovers, recentScans }`` for the authenticated user. Top movers are scored against the trailing 1-year price history embedded in each card's metadata; recent scans are the last N graded cards in scan-time order. Both rails are bounded by the optional ``topMovers`` and ``recentScans`` query params.

schema.title: Response Get Home Feed V1 Home Feed Get

get.description:
Fetch and re-emit ``u`` as an image with long cache headers.

Returns the raw image bytes on success. On upstream failure returns
HTTP 502 — the mobile ``CardImage`` already retries failed loads, so
a transient blip will recover automatically.

Returns a normalized (first bucket = 100) index series for the named cohort. Currently the only supported `index_id` is `psa10` — the average PSA-10 spot price across every catalog card owned by at least one Loupe user. Use as a faded baseline overlay on the portfolio chart so users can read their relative performance vs. the broader PSA-10 market.

schema.title: Response Get Index History V1 Market Indices  Index Id  History Get
Response Start Checkout V1 Me Billing Checkout Post

post.summary: Begin a hosted Loupe Pro checkout

schema.title: Response Get Billing Config V1 Me Billing Config Get
Response Billing Portal V1 Me Billing Portal Post

post.summary: Open the Stripe customer portal (manage/cancel Pro)

schema.title: Response Subscribe V1 Me Billing Subscribe Post

post.summary: Create a subscription for the in-app Payment Element

get.summary: The signed-in user's effective Loupe Pro access

put.summary: Replace my recents (client sends the merged, capped list)

get.description:
Look up a PSA cert via the PSA Public API.

Free PSA tier is capped at 100 requests/day across the whole app, so
results are cached in Redis for 24h. Auth required so anonymous traffic
can't burn the quota.

schema.title: Response Verify Psa Cert V1 Providers Psa Cert  Cert No  Get

get.summary: Verify a PSA cert number (authed; cached 24h)

get.description: Return per-provider configuration + supported capabilities.

schema.title: Response Get Status V1 Providers Status Get

get.description:
Page through an entire game's upstream catalog (real ``total`` for paging),
sorted server-side. With ``set`` (e.g. ``pokemontcg:base1``) the page is scoped
to a single set. Unsupported games return an empty page rather than an error.

schema.title: Response Public Browse V1 Public Browse Get

get.summary: Browse a whole game catalog (paginated thousands of cards)

get.description:
Search (or, with no query, browse trending) with all derivation done here.

Returns the paginated slice plus ``total`` and ``facets`` so the client
renders directly — no client-side filtering/sorting/pagination.

schema.title: Response Public Search V1 Public Search Get

get.summary: Public storefront search (server-side filter / sort / paginate / facets)

get.description:
A tiny trend series per id so list rows (search dropdown, rails) can
show a Robinhood-style sparkline without one request per card.

Reuses the cached, synthesized price history, so a batch of visible ids
resolves from cache after the first hit. Capped to keep the fan-out small.

schema.title: Response Public Sparklines V1 Public Sparklines Get

get.summary: Batch mini price series for a set of cards (list-row sparklines)

get.description:
Trending feed with the cut applied server-side.

``sort=value`` ("most valuable") draws from a dedicated high-priced source —
not a re-sort of the newest-cards pool — so the rail is genuinely valuable.
Every returned card is guaranteed to have a price *and* art, so the UI never
renders a "—" priceless tile or a bare image.

schema.title: Response Public Trending V1 Public Trending Get
Response List Mine V1 Reports Get

post.summary: Generate a monthly or yearly statement

get.description:
Return the next monthly and yearly close datetimes.

Statements are generated automatically — the client uses this to
render "Your next statement closes on …" instead of asking the
user to tap a button. The endpoint is pure date math (no DB hit
beyond auth) so it stays cheap even when polled.

schema.title: Response Upcoming V1 Reports Upcoming Get

get.summary: When my next statements will auto-close
Short-lived presigned URL for the PDF
Stream the PDF (fallback when presigning isn't available)

schema.title: Response List Scanners V1 Scanners Get

get.description: Returns the scanner with the most-recent `last_seen_at` for the signed-in user, or `null` when the user has never paired one. Frontend uses this to render the 'Scanner connection' widget on the Command Center without requiring the user to pick a specific device id.

schema.title: Response Get Status V1 Scanners Status Get
Response List Scans V1 Scans Get

post.summary: Create a new scan job and return presigned upload URLs
Mark uploads complete; enqueue grading worker

get.description: Returns the signed-in user's sealed inventory, joined to the catalog row so the client can render product name / image without an N+1 round-trip. Soft-deleted rows are excluded; use `include_opened=false` to also hide boxes the user has marked as opened.

schema.title: Response List Mine V1 Sealed Holdings Get

get.description: Free-text search across the global sealed-product catalog (booster boxes, ETBs, tins, etc.). Optional `tcg` and `product_type` filters. Public — no auth required so unsigned browse flows work.

schema.title: Response Search Catalog V1 Sealed Search Get

get.description: Current low/mid/high/market for one sealed product, plus its MSRP. The headline market comes from TCGplayer (richest quote) and falls back to PriceCharting's structured per-SKU match when TCGplayer's fuzzy catalog name doesn't resolve. Sealed SKUs have no stored history, so this is a point-in-time snapshot; price fields are null when no source resolves a quote.
get.summary: Live market snapshot for a sealed product

schema.title: Response List Sets V1 Sets Get

get.description: Returns one entry per set the user owns at least one card from, sorted by completion percent (highest first). Each entry: `{setId, setName, setCode, tcg, imageUrl, owned, total, percent, estimatedValueUsd, missingTop: [{cardId, name, number, imageUrl}]}`. All values are computed from real graded-card data; sets with unknown total fall back to the count of cards we have indexed.

schema.title: Response Get Progress V1 Sets Progress Get

get.summary: Set-completion progress for the signed-in user

get.description: Returns every card the signed-in user has pinned, most-recent first.

schema.title: Response List Mine V1 Watchlist Get

post.description: Idempotent. Returns 201 with the existing row when the card was already pinned.
post.summary: Pin a card to my watchlist

delete.summary: Unpin a card from my watchlist
